content.claim: Early tracking via 6- and 8-year gimnázium admission at ages 10-11 and 12-13 is independently linked to major educational inequality beyond prior ability differences, with secondary school tracks highly stratified by family socioeconomic status and affecting later opportunities.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Finding disentangles tracking effect from SES in authors' models but represents one research lineage rather than uncontested consensus
Does not isolate the specific causal share of 6/8-year gimnázium versus broader 3-track secondary structure
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
